comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Mechanism (verified from code, root cause of the crash localized): validate_to() in src/rebar/llm/structured.py:180-188 unwraps a top-level JSON array ONLY when it contains exactly ONE dict element (prior fix artsy-chain-hold/dash-lure-slag/slit-rubble-braid). The completion-verifier's Pass-3 'decide' step here returned a list with zero OR two-or-more dict elements, which is deliberately treated as ambiguous and raises 'structured output must be a JSON object, got list' -> fail-closed close block. So this is a MODEL-reliability defect (decide step emitting a non-single-object array) surfacing as a fail-closed gate crash.

To fix CORRECTLY needs the ACTUAL failing model output (zero-dict vs multi-dict list) — not captured here — plus a judgment call: (a) harden the decide step's prompt/schema to force a single verdict object, and/or (b) add a bounded-retry that feeds 'return a single JSON object' back, and/or (c) verdict-specific salvage (pick the element carrying a 'verdict' key) — the last would leak model-specifics into the generic validate_to, so likely belongs at the decide-step layer. NOT fixing speculatively without the captured output; needs a focused follow-up with a real reproduction (a billable verify-completion run on a weak-AC ticket).

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Ensure verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true (on for this repo) with the [agents] extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
2. Close a Jira-imported bug whose description is an error dump with no well-formed '## Acceptance Criteria' block (observed on jira-reb-714): `rebar transition jira-reb-714 in_progress closed --reason 'Fixed: ...'`.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: the completion verifier produces a PASS/FAIL verdict (or degrades gracefully), so a resolved ticket can be closed and signed.
- Actual: the verify workflow raises 'completion verification could not run (... step "decide@then/verify" failed: structured output must be a JSON object, got list)'. Per the fail-closed close gate this BLOCKS the close (exit 1) even when the bug is genuinely resolved. Only --force-close works, which loses the signed attestation.
- A well-formed ticket (cc7f) closed via the verifier fine, so the crash is triggered by ticket shape (weak/absent AC), not universal.

## Root Cause (hypothesis)

The completion workflow's 'decide@then/verify' step expects a JSON object but the model returned a JSON list; the workflow validates and raises instead of coercing/repairing, turning a recoverable output-shape mismatch into a fail-closed crash of the close gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The 'decide' step tolerates/coerces an array-vs-object structured-output mismatch (or the schema/prompt guarantees an object), producing a verdict instead of crashing.
- [ ] Closing a resolved ticket with a weak/absent AC yields a PASS/FAIL verdict, not a fail-closed 'could not run' crash.
- [ ] Regression test covers the decide-step output-shape mismatch.
compiled_state.title: completion-verifier close gate crashes fail-closed: decide@then/verify — 'structured output must be a JSON object, got list'
